commit.message:
Add parallel subagents and scheduled autonomous agents

Parallel subagents:
- /obsidian-save spawns per-type agents (people, projects, tasks, decisions, ideas)
- /obsidian-health spawns per-category agents (links, duplicates, frontmatter, staleness, orphans)
- /obsidian-recap spawns one agent per daily note for parallel reading
- /obsidian-init spawns agents for dashboard, templates, boards, and note samples simultaneously

Scheduled autonomous agents:
- obsidian-morning: daily 8am — creates daily note, surfaces overdue tasks
- obsidian-nightly: daily 10pm — closes out the day, appends summary
- obsidian-weekly: Fridays 6pm — generates weekly review note
- obsidian-health-check: Sundays 9pm — runs health check, saves report only

README updated with new Features entries and Scheduled Agents section with setup instructions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: AuthGate was redirecting all patient tabs back to dashboard

The inAuthFlow check matched all /patient/* routes, so navigating to
Bookings, Records, Pharmacy, or Profile immediately triggered a redirect
back to the home tab. Now inAuthFlow only matches the splash screen and
login/OTP screens, and adds proper redirect-to-login for expired tokens
on protected routes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: handle Windows Terminal trailing whitespace stripping in paste events (#58)

Windows Terminal strips trailing whitespace from each line and trailing
newlines when forwarding clipboard content via bracketed paste. This
caused _on_paste to treat the paste as external, overwriting the local
clipboard (losing trailing spaces) and resetting the line-copy flag
(breaking VS Code line-paste behavior).

Use a trailing-whitespace-tolerant comparison (rstrip per line after
rstrip on the whole string) so that whitespace-only differences
preserve the local clipboard and line-copy state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Overhaul scripts, add experiment tracking, and document organization

Scripts:
- Replace nemo_sif_exec/shell and vllm_v012_exec/shell with unified
  nemo_sif.sh and vllm_sif.sh (no-args = interactive shell, args = exec)
- Remove vllm patch file binds (patches didn't work)
- Remove old Option A pytorch-devel SIF scripts (build_sif, sif_exec,
  sif_shell, eval_slurm, train_slurm, install_deps, setup_and_eval)
- Update all references across SLURM scripts, Python files, and docs

Eval:
- Switch evaluation to vLLM offline inference (run_eval_vllm.py)
- Remove old run_eval.py (transformers-based, superseded)
- Auto-name preds files with SLURM_JOB_ID or timestamp prefix

Experiment tracking:
- Add --experiment flag to train.py, train_grpo.py, run_eval_vllm.py
- Prefixes all artifact names: nemotron-lora/<exp>_<job_id>/,
  preds/<exp>_<job_id>_*.json
- Auto-writes JSON manifest to experiments/<exp>/ on completion
  (adapter path, accuracy scores, config snapshot)
- Add experiments/ to .gitignore

Docs:
- Add CLAUDE.md with full architecture, common commands, experiment
  best practices, eval config reference, and project layout
- Add EXPERIMENTS.md and RESULTS.md (experiment log + results table)
- Add GRPO trainer (train_grpo.py), prompt strategy hints (prompts.py)
- Add configs: base.yaml updates, eval_subset.json, eval_subset_by_qtype.json

Update README with commands table and revised examples

fix: corrigir 5 pontos críticos de segurança e confiabilidade

1. SECRET_KEY: warn em qualquer env, process.exit apenas em produção
2. Rate limiting: por tenant (JWT decode) em vez de só por IP; webhooks isentos
3. Webhook async: responde 200 imediatamente, processa em background (evita timeout)
4. Task Worker: migra de setInterval para BullMQ repeat job (persistência + retry)
5. JWT Refresh Token: endpoint POST /auth/refresh, refresh_token salvo no login,
   AuthContext tenta renovar automaticamente antes de fazer logout no 401

feat: agentic notification system + autonomous outreach pipeline

Phase 1A — In-app notifications:
- user_notifications table with index on (user_id, is_read, created_at)
- _create_notification() helper, 3 API endpoints (list, mark-read, unread-count)
- Hooked into underwrite completion, high-score scan deals, and outreach sends
- Frontend: notification bell with badge, dropdown panel, 60s polling, mark-all-read

Phase 1B — Email digest:
- email_digest and digest_email columns on scan_profiles
- _send_email_digest() gathers new deals, underwrite results, outreach activity
- HTML email builder, SMTP sender, 24h scheduler job

Phase 2A — Auto skip-trace + auto outreach:
- auto_skip_trace, auto_outreach, template/channel/delay/min_score columns
- _do_skip_trace() reusable helper extracted from endpoint
- Auto skip-trace runs after underwrite; auto outreach queued with review window
- _dispatch_queued_outreach() hourly job sends after delay expires
- Rate limiting (10/hr), notification on queue and send

Phase 2B — Follow-up sequences:
- outreach_sequences table (position, max_steps, days_between, next_send_at)
- Sequence auto-created on any outreach send (manual or auto)
- _dispatch_follow_ups() 6-hourly job sends follow-up template
- Auto-completes sequence on deal outcome

Profile CRUD updated with all new fields. Scanner UI updated with
auto-outreach settings section in profile modal.

commit.message:
Win32: wire Phase 7 input-equality short-circuiting

Early return in rebuild() when inputsUnchanged(snapshot:) is true.
Placed before WM_SETREDRAW suppression — no painting, input state,
or body evaluation work is done at all.

If a rebuild is triggered but none of the storages read during the
last body evaluation changed their generation counter, the entire
rebuild is skipped. Matches GTK4/Web behavior.

Manually verified:
- Stopwatch timer ticks
- ColorMixer sliders update
- ParityStateData counter works

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Win32: wire Phase 6 dependency tracking into ViewHost

Win32ViewHost now conforms to DependencyTrackingHost. Body evaluation
is wrapped in beginDependencyTracking/endDependencyTracking in both
rebuild() and initial render (winRenderStatefulView).

This is behavior-affecting: @Published changes for storages not read
during body evaluation no longer trigger rebuilds. Matches GTK4/Web.

lastInputSnapshot is captured but not yet used for Phase 7
short-circuiting (separate step).

Manually verified:
- Stopwatch timer ticks (elapsed IS read -> rebuilds correctly)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix accent text colour to follow user's primary colour (#62)

--accent-primary1-text (used by links, active tabs, interactive text)
was hardcoded to teal (#00737F) in tokens.css and never updated when
the user changed their primary colour. Now generated dynamically:
- Light mode: darkened version of primary that meets WCAG AA (4.5:1)
  contrast on white backgrounds
- Dark mode: uses the primary colour directly (light on dark)

commit.message:
Remove noisy auto-connect and echo from WSL shell startup

- Remove "wsl detected" echo (redundant with .zshrc guard)
- Remove backgrounded adb connect commands that ran on every shell open
  (adb.exe one would suspend on tty input; both added job control noise)
- Device aliases (adb-p7, adb-p9, etc.) still available for on-demand use

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: zombie horde timeout — keep alive while zombies move, increase to 5min

Root cause: horde.last_attack_at was only updated when player hit a zombie.
While zombies walked 700m toward player (takes >2min), the horde timed out.

Fix:
- Update horde.last_attack_at every tick while zombies are active/moving
- Increase timeout from 2min to 5min
- Removed debug logs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: media files not served — switch from named volume to bind mount

Docker named volume media_data was not accessible to host nginx.
Changed to bind mount ./media:/app/media so nginx can serve files
directly from /opt/kizuna-crm/media/.

* feat(ptrace): wire soft-delete through ptrace handler

The ptrace tracer's softDeleteFile() was already implemented and tested
but the handler denied DecisionSoftDelete decisions because the trash
directory wasn't available in the handler context.

Pass trash directory from FUSE audit config (sandbox.fuse.audit.trash_path)
into ptraceHandlerRouter. When configured, DecisionSoftDelete now returns
the soft-delete action with TrashDir, enabling the tracer to intercept
unlinkat and inject mkdirat + renameat2 to move files to trash.

Falls back to deny when no trash directory is configured.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ptrace): resolve relative trash path to absolute

Relative trash paths (default: .agentsh_trash) are resolved against
sessions base dir at init time, not the tracee's CWD at runtime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ptrace): ensure trash path is always absolute via filepath.Abs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ptrace): fail closed on filepath.Abs error for trash path

Log warning and disable soft-delete instead of silently keeping
a potentially relative path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ptrace): resolve trash path per-session against workspace

Resolve relative trash paths against the session workspace (s.Workspace)
at HandleFile time, matching purgeTrashForSession behavior exactly.
This ensures ptrace soft-delete writes and session purge target the
same directory.

Previously resolved at init time against Sessions.BaseDir which could
differ from the per-session workspace used by purge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ptrace): default trash path to .agentsh_trash matching purge behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ptrace): normalize resolved trash path with filepath.Abs

Fail closed if absolute path resolution fails.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ptrace): check PolicyDecision for soft-delete, not EffectiveDecision

The policy engine maps DecisionSoftDelete → EffectiveDecision=allow,
so the EffectiveDecision switch never reached the soft-delete case.
Check PolicyDecision before the switch to correctly intercept
soft-delete decisions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ptrace): unify trash path resolution and scope soft-delete to delete ops

Extract shared resolveTrashPath() used by both ptrace soft-delete write
and session purge cleanup, ensuring both target the same directory.
Gate soft-delete routing to fc.Operation == "delete" so non-unlink file
operations fall through to normal allow handling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(test): use platform-portable paths in resolveTrashPath tests

Use runtime.GOOS to select absolute path roots so tests pass on
Windows where filepath.IsAbs rejects POSIX-style paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(ptrace): add handler-level soft-delete tests and fix test isolation

Add TestHandleFile_SoftDelete covering:
- delete op with configured trash → soft-delete action
- non-delete op with soft_delete policy → falls through to allow
- empty trash dir → deny (fail-closed)

Remove os.Chdir from resolve_trash_test to avoid process-wide side effects.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ptrace): include rmdir in soft-delete gate to prevent bypass

unlinkat(AT_REMOVEDIR) maps to operation "rmdir", not "delete".
Without this, directory removals with a soft_delete policy would
fall through to EffectiveDecision=allow, causing a hard delete.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: zombie markers normal entities, accurate haversine distance, attacks work

Frontend:
- Removed CSS transition on markers (caused floating over map on zoom)
- Removed sway/glow animations (caused lag)
- Plain setLatLng like goblins/defenders
- Boss keeps green drop-shadow, no animation

Backend:
- Fixed distance calculation: was using approxDist = sqrt(dLat²+dLng²)*111320
  which is WRONG for longitude (1° lng ≠ 111km at high latitudes).
  Now uses haversine() for accurate distance. This is why zombies never
  reached attack range (450m) — approx distance was inflated ~1.7x at lat 55°
- Movement uses simple dLat/dLng ratio (correct for small steps)

commit.message:
Dispersion scaling results: v ∝ k^0.74 → 0 (massive behavior)

Key findings at small θ with dual parity coin:
- v(k) follows power law v ≈ a·k^0.74 at small k (large σ)
- v→0 as k→0: the walk IS massive, not asymptotically massless
- Exponent α≈0.74 for both θ=0.005 and θ=0.01
- This is a fractional dispersion, not standard Dirac (which gives α=1)
- At large θ (≥0.1), mass effect screens and v→constant

Data (dual parity coin, σ=1000-10000):
  θ=0.005: v = 30.2·k^0.734
  θ=0.01:  v = 20.3·k^0.743

Coin_type=5 (f1+f2+e·α triple coin): v≈0.521 constant (massless)
Coin_type=4 (f1+f2+β): v increases, no mass
Only the dual parity coin (type=3) gives mass at long wavelengths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add input-equality short-circuiting via generation counters

Skips body evaluation when no storage in the host's read-set has
been mutated since the last render. StateStorage and PublishedStorage
each maintain a generation counter incremented on every setValue().
Snapshots capture weak storage refs + generations during tracking.
Before rebuild, inputsUnchanged() compares current generations to
the snapshot — if all match, body evaluation is skipped.

Empty snapshots always return false (rebuild), preventing the
short-circuit from blocking @Observable or @FocusState driven
rebuilds which don't use GenerationTracked storages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: collection applies clan bonus to income RATE, not rounded accumulation

Was: calcAccumulatedCoins(coreBoost) → round → * clanBonus → round
Now: calcAccumulatedCoins(coreBoost * mineCountBoost * clanBonus)

This matches the tick income calculation in map.js (lines 519-534)
where clan bonus is applied to the per-second rate before accumulation.
Eliminates double-rounding that caused displayed > collected amounts.

Add "gap" and "missing" to easy mode banned vocabulary, fix skill instructions

"Gap" is verdict language — "One additional gap was found" is the
opposite of easy mode's observational tone. The di-pr-review skill
used "gap" in its own instructions ("Identify the gap between..."),
training Claude to think in gap-framing. This leaked into PR
descriptions even when easy mode was active.

- docs/EASY_MODE_LANGUAGE.md: add "gap"/"gap was found"/"gap in
  coverage" and "missing" (as verdict) to the softening vocabulary
  table with observational alternatives
- skills/di-pr-review/SKILL.md: reword two instructions that used
  "gap" as framing (lines 3016, 4608)
- analyses/process-easy-mode-session-scope.md: filed postmortem

commit.message:
fix: derive streets from actual section elements, not grid formulas

Measure getBoundingClientRect() of each grid child, group into rows
by Y position, find real pixel gaps between section edges. Streets
are midpoints of actual spaces between windows. Handles expanded
sections gracefully — if a street disappears, ants remap to
available streets. No coordinate assumptions or grid math.
